title: Pre-flight must gate on post-primary event count, not just regime-mask coverage
source: Manual M3 audit of T011D2 (2026-05-27): proposal passed pre-flight then died in the audit subprocess on the walk-forward refusal cliff.
why: T011D2 passed pre-flight on regime-mask coverage (2641/5638 bars = 46.8% in BULL_QUIET) but its primary fired only 35 triple-barrier events over 22y. That is below both the proposal's own committed falsification floor (n_trades_total_min >= 75) and the hardcoded walk-forward geometry minimum in pipeline/walk_forward.py (train_min + n_folds*100). The audit therefore burned a full subprocess only to raise WalkForwardGeometryError. Pre-flight should compute the post-primary, in-regime event count and fail fast when it is below max(proposal.n_trades_total_min, walk-forward geometry minimum), surfacing it as a falsification rather than a subprocess_failed. CONCRETE EVIDENCE 2026-05-27 (tick 15 / T015D2M): pre-flight PASSED because the proposal n_trades_total_min was 50 and the gate only checks that, but the full audit died on the WF refusal cliff at 73 events < 236 required (train_min 36 + 2 folds x 100). A whole materialization+adversarial-review cycle was spent on a proposal that pre-flight should have flagged as WF-infeasible. The pre-flight event-count gate must project against the WF geometry minimum (train_min + n_folds x test_pool), not just the proposal-declared n_trades_total_min.
value_rationale: H: cheap to add, prevents wasted 10-30min audit subprocesses, and converts an opaque subprocess_failed into an honest, early falsification signal for the whole Loop A family.
scope: Add a post-primary event-count check to the pre-flight stage (scripts/loop_a_tick.py run_preflight and/or phase5.run_proposal).
Count non-zero primary signals within the regime mask after triple-barrier labeling.
Record the outcome as preflight_failed with reason='event_floor', not subprocess_failed.

history.reason: chore(backlog): sync registry â€” positive-control + meta diagnosis (B0061-B0064)

Implemented the event-floor gate: pure resolve_train_min + wf_event_floor in pipeline/walk_forward.py (TDD, anti-drift test ties them to make_folds boundary); evaluate_event_floor decision fn + --preflight-event-count orchestrator mode + gate in phase5.run_proposal before the heavy subprocess. Recorded as status=event_floor (cleaner than overloading preflight_failed) and exits 0 as an honest falsification. Verified end-to-end on T015D2M: 73 events < 300 floor -> event_floor, no training subprocess run. NOTE: gate lives in the full-audit path, not --preflight-only, because Loop A customs are unmaterialized at preflight (no module to count); adding it there would be dead code.
